get.description:
**Admin access required**  
Retrieve a list of all wireless connection types in the system.  
Supports search filtering and pagination via limit/offset.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of wireless types to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of wireless types to return

post.description:
**Admin access required**  
Create a new wireless connection type in the system.  
Intended for system administrators managing the available wireless types.

get.summary: Get wireless type by ID (Admin only)
get.description:
**Admin access required**  
Retrieve detailed information for a specific wireless connection type by its unique ID.
Useful for viewing configuration, metadata, and usage details.

put.summary: Update wireless type by ID (Admin only)
put.description:
**Admin access required**  
Update the details of an existing wireless connection type by its unique ID.  
Intended for system administrators to modify configuration or metadata.

delete.summary: Delete wireless type by ID (Admin only)
delete.description:
**Admin access required**  
Delete a specific wireless connection type by its unique ID.  
Use with caution — this action is irreversible and will remove the type from the system.

get.description:
**Admin access required**  
Retrieve a paginated list of products in the system.  
Supports optional filtering by name, category, or other attributes, and pagination via limit/offset.

post.description:
**Admin access required**  
Create a new product entry in the system.  
Intended for system administrators to manage the product catalog.

**Admin access required**  
Upload or replace the primary image for a specific product, identified by its unique ID.  
The uploaded image will be stored and linked to the product record in the system.

get.description:
**Admin access required**  
Retrieve detailed information for a specific product by its unique ID.  
Useful for viewing full product data, including attributes, pricing, images, and metadata.

put.description:
**Admin access required**  
Update the details of an existing product by its unique ID.  
Intended for system administrators to modify product information such as name, description, pricing, inventory, or category assignments.

delete.description:
**Admin access required**  
Delete a specific product by its unique ID.  
This action is irreversible — once deleted, the product will be permanently removed from the system.

get.summary: Get list of factory devices (Admin only)
get.description:
**Admin access required**
Retrieve all registered factory devices in the system.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of factory devices to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of factory devices to return

parameters.name: dev_eui

schema.description: Filter factory devices by Device EUI
schema.title: Dev Eui

parameters.description: Filter factory devices by Device EUI

post.summary: Create a new factory device (Admin only)
post.description:
**Admin access required**
Create and register a new factory device in the system.

get.description:
**Admin access required**
Retrieve detailed information for a specific factory device by its ID.

put.description:
**Admin access required**
Update configuration or status of a specific factory device.

delete.description:
**Admin access required**
Permanently remove a specific factory device from the system.

get.description:
Public access — No authentication required.
Retrieve a list of all available application templates in the system.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of application templates to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of application templates to return

post.summary: Create a new application template (Admin only)
post.description:
**Admin access required**  
Create and register a new application template in the system.  
Intended for system administrators to define available application types.

**Admin access required**  
Upload or replace the image associated with a specific application template.
This is typically used to update the visual branding of a template.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information for a specific application template by its ID.  
This endpoint is **public** and does not require authentication.

put.description:
**Admin access required**  
Update the details or configuration of an existing application template.
Intended for system administrators to manage available templates.

delete.description:
**Admin access required**  
Delete an existing application template from the system.  
This operation is irreversible and should be performed with caution.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of applications visible to the current user.  
Accessible by both **Admin** and **Tenant** accounts.

post.description:
Create a new application in the system.  
Accessible by both **Admin** and **Tenant** accounts with appropriate permissions.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information for a specific application.  
Accessible by both **Admin** and **Tenant** accounts with the appropriate permissions.

put.description:
Update an existing application in the system.  
Accessible by both **Admin** and **Tenant** accounts with the appropriate permissions.

delete.description:
Delete an existing application from the system.  
Accessible by both **Admin** and **Tenant** accounts with appropriate permissions.  
This operation is irreversible.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of available device profile templates in the system.

Create a new device profile template in the system.  
**Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.  
Requires JSON body containing the template details.

post.summary: Upload device profile template image (Admin only)

Upload or replace the image for a specific device profile template.  
The image URL will be stored inside the 'tags' JSONB column.

get.summary: Get device profile template by ID (Admin only)
get.description:

put.description:

delete.summary: Delete a device profile template by ID (Admin only)
delete.description:

get.summary: List device profiles (Admin & Tenant with access)

delete.summary: Delete a device profile by ID (Admin & Tenant with access)

Accessible by **Admin** or **Tenant** users with permission to the tenant that owns the device profile.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of gateways with optional filters.

**Access control:**
- **Global Admin**: Can list all gateways; `tenantId` can be omitted.
- **Tenant User**: Must provide a `tenantId` they have access to.

**Features:**
- Pagination with `limit` and `offset`
- Search by gateway name
- Filter by tenant

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of wireless types to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of wireless types to return

parameters.name: tenantId

schema.description: Filter gateway by tenant ID. To list all gateways as a global admin user, this field can be left blank
schema.title: Tenantid

parameters.description: Filter gateway by tenant ID. To list all gateways as a global admin user, this field can be left blank

post.summary: Create a new gateway (Admin or Tenant Gateway Admin)
post.description:
Create a new gateway in the system.

**Access control:**
- **Global Admin**: Can create a gateway for any tenant
- **Tenant Admin** or **Tenant Gateway Admin**: Can create a gateway for their own tenant
- **Tenant User** without admin privileges: Not authorized to create a gateway

**Notes:**
- Non-admin users must have `is_admin` or `is_gateway_admin` set to `True` in their tenant membership.
- Tenant ID must be specified as part of the gateway creation request.

put.summary: Update an existing gateway (Admin or Tenant Gateway Admin)
put.description:
Update details of an existing gateway.

**Access control:**
- **Global Admin**: Can update any gateway
- **Tenant Admin** or **Tenant Gateway Admin**: Can update gateways within their own tenant
- **Tenant User** without admin privileges: Not authorized to update gateways

**Notes:**
- Non-admin users must have `is_admin` or `is_gateway_admin` set to `True` in their tenant membership.
- The gateway ID must belong to a gateway under the tenant the user has permission to manage.

delete.summary: Delete an existing gateway (Admin or Tenant Gateway Admin)
delete.description:
Delete a gateway from the system.

**Access control:**
- **Global Admin**: Can delete any gateway
- **Tenant Admin** or **Tenant Gateway Admin**: Can delete gateways within their own tenant
- **Tenant User** without admin privileges: Not authorized to delete gateways

**Notes:**
- Non-admin users must have `is_admin` or `is_gateway_admin` set to `True` in their tenant membership.
- The gateway ID must belong to a gateway under the tenant the user has permission to manage.

post.summary: Add a device to the multicast group.

delete.summary: Remove a device from the multicast group.

get.summary: List the items in the multicast group queue.

post.summary: Add the given item to the multicast group queue.

get.summary: Get status of solar street light devices (Admin & Tenant with access)
get.description:
Retrieve the latest operational status for solar street light devices.

**Access control:**
- **Admin** users: Can view all devices
- **Tenant** users: Can view devices belonging to their accessible tenants

**Features:**
- Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset`
- Optionally filter by `templateId` to retrieve devices of a specific application template
- Each device status includes essential telemetry and metadata for operational monitoring

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Limit the number of devices returned
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Limit the number of devices returned

get.summary: Get active alerts for solar street light devices (Admin & Tenant with access)
get.description:
Retrieve a list of devices that have active alerts or warning conditions.

**Access control:**
- **Admin** users: Can view alerts for all devices
- **Tenant** users: Can view alerts for devices belonging to their accessible tenants

**Features:**
- Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset`
- `limit` cannot exceed **200** records per request
- Optionally filter by `templateId` to narrow down to a specific application template
- Each alert includes device metadata, last seen time, SOC (State of Charge), and alert status

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Limit the number of devices returned
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Limit the number of devices returned

get.summary: Get latest device record for a solar street light (Admin & Tenant with access)
get.description:
Retrieve the most recent device record for a specific solar street light identified by its DevEUI.

Accessible by:
- **Admin** users (all devices)
- **Tenant** users with permission to the tenant that owns the device

If the device belongs to a tenant the current user does not have access to, a **403 Forbidden** will be returned.

parameters.name: devEui

schema.description: Hexadecimal DevEUI of the solar street light device
schema.title: Deveui

parameters.description: Hexadecimal DevEUI of the solar street light device

get.summary: Get device records in a time range (Admin & Tenant with access)
get.description:
Retrieve device records for a specific solar street light (by DevEUI) within a given time range.

Accessible by:
- **Admin** users (access to all devices)
- **Tenant** users with permission to the tenant that owns the device

Supports:
- Time range filtering (`startTime`, `endTime`)
- Pagination with `limit` and `offset`
- Sorted ascending by timestamp

schema.description: Hexadecimal DevEUI of the solar street light
schema.title: Deveui

parameters.description: Hexadecimal DevEUI of the solar street light

schema.description: Max number of records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of records to return

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all tenants in the system.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Returns tenant metadata for system-wide administration
- Can be extended to support pagination, search, or filtering if required

post.description:
Create a new tenant in the system.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Accepts tenant details for registration
- Returns the newly created tenant ID
- Designed to be extensible for additional setup hooks (e.g., default roles, permissions)

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all tenants in the system.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Returns tenant metadata for system-wide administration

get.summary: List tenant users with tenant and user info

get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific tenant by its ID.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Returns full tenant metadata for administrative purposes
- Useful for viewing tenant-specific configuration or status

put.description:
Update details of a specific tenant by its ID.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Allows updating tenant metadata (e.g., name, contact info, status)
- Returns the updated tenant details

delete.description:
Delete a specific tenant by its ID.

**Access control:**  
- **Admin only** — this endpoint is restricted to administrator accounts.

**Features:**
- Permanently removes tenant record from the system
- Can be extended to handle cascading deletes (e.g., users, devices, configurations)

get.description:
Retrieve all users belonging to a specific tenant.

**Access control:**
- Any authenticated user who is a member of the tenant can view the list.
- Not restricted to administrators.

**Features:**
- Returns a list of users associated with the tenant
- Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset`
- Can be extended with filters (e.g., active status, roles)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of tenant users to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of tenant users to return

post.summary: Add an existing user to a tenant
post.description:
Assign an existing platform user to a specific tenant.

**Access control:**  
- **Global Admin** — Can add users to any tenant.  
- **Tenant Admin** — Can add users only to their own tenant.  

**Notes:**
- The user **must already exist** in the system before calling this API.
- Common use case: granting tenant membership to an existing user.
- Non-admin tenant members are not permitted to perform this action.

**Features:**
- Accepts a user ID and optional role flags for the target tenant.
- Validates both tenant and user existence.
- Can be extended to send invitation emails or assign default roles.

get.summary: Get user in a tenant by ID
get.description:
Retrieve details of a specific user that belongs to the given tenant.

**Features:**
- Returns user details along with tenant association
- Useful for viewing role assignments, permissions, or user status within the tenant

put.summary: Update a tenant user's roles and permissions
put.description:
Modify roles or permissions for an existing user within a specific tenant.

**Access control:**  
- **Global Admin** — Can update any tenant's users.  
- **Tenant Admin** — Can update users only in their own tenant.  

**Notes:**
- The target user must already be a member of the tenant.
- Non-admin tenant members cannot update other users' roles.
- This operation does not change the user's global account properties.

**Features:**
- Update role flags such as `is_admin`, `is_device_admin`, or `is_gateway_admin`.
- Validate that both tenant and user exist, and that membership exists.
- Prevent downgrading the last remaining admin in a tenant.

delete.summary: Remove a user from a tenant
delete.description:
Remove an existing user from a specific tenant.

**Access control:**  
- **Global Admin** — Can remove users from any tenant.  
- **Tenant Admin** — Can remove users only from their own tenant.  

**Notes:**
- The target user must already be a member of the tenant.
- Non-admin tenant members cannot perform this action.
- Cannot remove the last remaining tenant admin.

**Features:**
- Validates that the user and tenant exist, and that the membership exists.
- Optionally can cascade removal of related tenant-specific data (if implemented).
- Prevents accidental lockout of tenant by ensuring at least one admin remains.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all users in the system.

**Access Control:** Restricted to *Global Administrators* only.  
Admins have full system-wide access, including viewing all user accounts, roles, 
and statuses across all tenants.

**Parameters:**
- `limit` — Number of users to return per request (1–100, default: 10)  
- `offset` — Number of users to skip before returning results (default: 0)

**Typical use cases:**
- Auditing all system users  
- Reviewing role assignments and account statuses  
- Exporting user data for compliance checks  

**Security Note:** Handle returned data with appropriate safeguards, 
as it may contain sensitive account information.

post.description:
Create a new user account in the system.

**Access Control:** Restricted to *Global Administrators* only.  
Only admins have permission to create new accounts with defined roles and privileges.

**Request Body:**
- `name` — Full name of the user (max 100 characters)  
- `username` — Unique username for login (max 100 characters)  
- `email` — User email address (must be valid format)  
- `isActive` — Boolean indicating if the account is active (default: True)  
- `isAdmin` — Boolean flag for global admin privileges (default: False)  
- `note` — Optional notes about the user

**Typical use cases:**
- Onboarding new employees or platform members  
- Assigning initial permissions and roles  
- Preparing accounts for upcoming projects or teams

**Security Note:**  
- Be cautious when setting `isAdmin=True`.  
- Ensure the email and username are unique to prevent conflicts.

get.summary: Get user details by ID (Admin only)
get.description:
Retrieve detailed information for a specific user by their unique ID.

**Access Control:** Restricted to *Global Administrators* only.  
Admins have full system-wide access, including viewing complete profiles, 
roles, permissions, and account metadata.

**Path Parameter:**
- `userId` — The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

**Typical use cases:**
- Reviewing a specific user's account status and permissions  
- Auditing user activity for compliance purposes  
- Troubleshooting account-related issues

**Security Note:**  
The returned data may include sensitive information. Ensure proper handling
and compliance with your organization's data protection policies.

put.description:
Update the details of an existing user by their unique ID.

**Access Control:** Restricted to *Global Administrators* only.  
Only admins can modify user profile information, roles, and account status.

**Path Parameter:**
- `userId` — The unique identifier of the user to update.

**Request Body:**
- `name` — Full name of the user (max 100 characters)  
- `username` — Unique username for login (max 100 characters)  
- `email` — Valid email address  
- `isActive` — Boolean indicating if the account is active  
- `isAdmin` — Boolean flag for global admin privileges (default: False)  
- `note` — Optional notes or remarks

**Typical use cases:**
- Correcting typos or outdated profile data  
- Granting or revoking admin privileges  
- Activating or deactivating accounts

**Security Note:**  
- Be cautious when updating `isAdmin` privileges.  
- Ensure input validation is enforced to maintain data integrity.

delete.description:
Permanently remove a user account from the system.

**Access Control:** Restricted to *Global Administrators* only.  
Admins have full system-wide authority, including the ability to delete any user account across all tenants.

**Path Parameter:**
- `userId` — The unique identifier of the user to delete

**Typical use cases:**
- Deactivating accounts for employees who have left the organization  
- Removing test or duplicate accounts  
- Enforcing compliance or security policies

**Security Note:**  
- This operation is irreversible — deleted accounts cannot be recovered  
- Consider disabling the account (`isActive=False`) instead of deleting, if you want to retain historical data

get.description:
Retrieve the profile information of a specific user.

**Access Control:**
- A user can view **their own profile**.
- Administrators can view any user's profile.

**Path Parameter:**
- `userId` — The unique identifier of the target user.

**Returned Information:**
- Basic user details (name, email, status)
- Associated tenants with role flags (isAdmin, isDeviceAdmin, isGatewayAdmin)

**Error Codes:**
- `403 FORBIDDEN` — Attempt to view another user's profile without admin rights.
- `404 NOT FOUND` — No such user exists.

- STRING: E.g. a firmware version, true / false value.
